completed_tasks.name: Update DashboardScreen to use NavButton + on_event
Vendor textual-rs and patch default on_event to auto-dispatch key_bindings
Patch app.rs to fall back to screen root when no focused widget

remaining_tasks.name: Commit vendor/ directory and all modified files
Audit sub-screen raw Button usages that may also need NavButton

blockers.description: vendor/ directory is untracked — patches will be lost if cargo cache is cleared before commit
blockers.workaround: run: git add vendor/ && git commit

human_actions_pending.action: Commit vendor/ and modified source files
human_actions_pending.context: All changes are uncommitted; vendor/textual-rs/ is untracked and large

human_actions_pending.action: Run cargo run and manually test navigation
human_actions_pending.context: All tests pass but real terminal behavior with YubiKey/mock mode needs verification

decisions.decision: Vendor textual-rs locally rather than adding on_event to every screen
decisions.rationale: 13 screens would all need identical boilerplate; one upstream patch protects all current and future screens

decisions.decision: NavButton widget carries action string rather than per-button message types
decisions.rationale: Reuses existing on_action string dispatch; no need for 6 distinct structs

decisions.decision: Fallback to screen root (not skip) when focused_widget is None
decisions.rationale: Labels-only screens like HelpScreen must still receive key events for Escape/back

next_action: git add vendor/ src/tui/widgets/nav_button.rs src/tui/widgets/mod.rs src/tui/dashboard.rs Cargo.toml Cargo.lock .planning/ && git commit -m 'fix: button navigation and keyboard dispatch for all screens'
context_notes: The textual-rs framework had two event-dispatch bugs. Bug 1: key_bindings on screens were never checked because screens can't be focused — only the focused child widget's bindings were checked at the app level. Bug 2: screens with no focusable children (Labels-only) had focused_widget=None, causing the dispatcher to skip entirely. Both fixed by patching the vendored copy. NavButton was added to DashboardScreen to solve the problem of distinguishing which of 6 identical Button widgets was pressed (they all emit the same generic Pressed message with no identity). Sub-screens with buttons may need the same NavButton treatment.
